# Trunk Migration Config Schema

This document defines the YAML configuration schema produced by `trunk-discover` and consumed by all other trunk migration skills.

## File Location

`trunk-migration-config.yaml` in the repository root.

## Full Schema

```yaml
# ============================================================
# Trunk Migration Configuration
# Generated by: /dotnet:trunk-discover
# ============================================================

# --- Service Information (Required) ---
# Single-service repos: use `service:` block (type defaults to "webapi").
# Multi-service repos (multiple deployable functions/APIs in one repo): use `services:` list.
# See "Multi-Service Schema" section at the bottom for the services: structure.
service:
  name: ""                    # K8s service name (e.g., "clientbuilding-api")
  app_name: ""                # Short app name (e.g., "clientbuilding")
  display_name: ""            # Full display name (e.g., "Trigent.Platform.ClientBuildingService.Api")
  project_path: ""            # Path to API project (e.g., "src/Trigent.Platform.ClientBuildingService.Api")
  dll_path: ""                # Main DLL name (e.g., "Trigent.Platform.ClientBuildingService.Api.dll")
  solution_file: ""           # Solution file name (e.g., "Trigent.Platform.ClientBuildingService.sln")
  type: "webapi"              # "webapi" (default) | "functions-worker" | "functions-http"

# --- Jira (Required) ---
jira:
  ticket: ""                  # Jira ticket ID (e.g., "PLT-2763")

# --- Regions and Environments (Required) ---
regions:
  - us
  - uk

environments:
  lower:
    - dev
    - staging
  prod:
    - prod

# --- Region-Specific Configuration (Required) ---
region_config:
  us:
    domain: "trigent.com"
    dev:
      acr: ""                 # ACR registry name (e.g., "devcracrregistrytj")
      cluster: ""             # AKS cluster name (e.g., "dev-aks-akscluster-re3-001-yn")
      cluster_rg: ""          # AKS cluster resource group (e.g., "dev-rg-re3-mu")
      appconfig: ""           # Azure App Config name — leave empty if fetched from Key Vault at deploy time
      key_vault: ""           # Key Vault name (e.g., "trigent-intg-kv-dev-dr")
    staging:
      acr: ""
      cluster: ""
      cluster_rg: ""
      appconfig: ""
      key_vault: ""
    prod:
      acr: ""
      cluster: ""
      cluster_rg: ""
      appconfig: ""
      key_vault: ""
  uk:
    domain: "cpoms.net"
    dev:
      acr: ""
      cluster: ""
      cluster_rg: ""
      appconfig: ""
      key_vault: ""
    staging:
      acr: ""
      cluster: ""
      cluster_rg: ""
      appconfig: ""
      key_vault: ""
    prod:
      acr: ""
      cluster: ""
      cluster_rg: ""
      appconfig: ""
      key_vault: ""

# --- New Relic Naming (Required) ---
new_relic:
  # Pattern used only if app_names is empty. Suffixes: dev, stag, prod, devuk, staguk, produk
  app_name_pattern: "{display_name}-{suffix}"
  # PREFERRED: Explicit per-region/env names extracted from live manifests.
  # Populate from the actual NEW_RELIC_APP_NAME values in current k8s manifests
  # to preserve continuity with existing New Relic dashboards/alerts.
  # Single-service: "region-env" → "AppName-suffix"
  # Multi-service:  "service-name" → { "region-env" → "AppName-suffix" }
  app_names: {}               # e.g., us-dev: "Trigent.MyService-dev", us-stag: "Trigent.MyService-stag"

# --- Kubernetes (Required) ---
kubernetes:
  namespace: "platform"
  node_selector: "app2np"
  health_check_path: "/v1/health"
  port: 8080
  resources:
    limits:
      memory: "600Mi"
      cpu: "500m"
    requests:
      memory: "300Mi"
      cpu: "100m"
  hpa:
    lower:
      min_replicas: 1
      max_replicas: 4
    prod:
      min_replicas: 4
      max_replicas: 20

# --- Gateway (Required) ---
# The gateway controller used by the cluster for HTTPRoute resources.
# Detect from existing HTTPRoute manifests or ask user.
gateway:
  name: "gateway"             # Gateway resource name
  namespace: "azure-alb"      # Gateway resource namespace
  # Defaults to Azure ALB Gateway. Other options:
  # - Envoy Gateway: name: "eg", namespace: "envoy-gateway-system"
  # - Traefik: name: "traefik-gateway", namespace: "traefik"

# --- Review Apps (Required) ---
review:
  domain: "review.trigent.com"
  cluster: ""                 # Dev cluster name for review apps
  cluster_rg: ""              # Dev cluster resource group
  key_vault: ""               # Key Vault name (e.g., "${KV_NAME}")
  resources:
    limits:
      memory: "300Mi"
      cpu: "200m"
    requests:
      memory: "100Mi"
      cpu: "50m"

# --- Decision Trees (Required) ---
decisions:
  # Decision Tree 1: API Versioning
  api_versioning:
    multi_version: false      # true if service exposes v1 + v2
    versions:
      - v1                    # List all API versions
    # API_IDs are existing values from Azure APIM — ask the user
    api_ids: {}               # Map of "region-env-version" → existing API_ID

  # Decision Tree 2: Auth0
  auth0:
    enabled: false            # true if auth0/ directory exists
    # secret_name: "APIKeys--authenticationPrimaryKey"  # Key Vault secret name

  # Decision Tree 3: NuGet Packages
  nuget:
    enabled: false            # true if .Client/.Shared/.Maps projects exist
    packages: []              # e.g., ["Client", "Shared", "Maps"]

  # Decision Tree 4: UI Regression Tests
  ui_tests:
    enabled: false            # true if service has UI regression tests in qa-automation
    trigger_project: ""       # GitLab project path for QA automation (e.g., "trigent1/trigent/quality-assurance/qa-automation/web-ui-framework-2-platform")
    schedule_name: ""         # QA framework schedule name for staging (e.g., "CICD - Client Building Service - P0,P1 - Staging")
    schedule_name_prod: ""    # QA framework schedule name for prod (e.g., "CICD - Client Building Service - P0,P1 - Prod")

  # Decision Tree 5: EF Migrations
  ef_migrations:
    enabled: false            # true if DataMigrations project exists
    db_context_name: ""       # e.g., "ClientsDBContext"
    connection_string_key: "" # e.g., "ClientsDB_Failover"
    data_project_path: ""     # e.g., "src/Trigent.Platform.ClientBuildingService.DataMigrations/..."

# --- Functions-Specific Configuration (Optional — Azure Functions repos only) ---
# Present when service.type is "functions-worker" or "functions-http" (or services list contains such types).
# For multi-service repos, each service in the services: list carries its own functions: block.
functions:
  secret_name: ""             # K8s secret name created at deploy time (e.g., "webhooks-processor-secrets")
  service_bus_secret_name: "" # Key Vault secret name for the primary Service Bus connection string (if used)
  keda:
    enabled: false            # true if service is scaled by KEDA
    # triggers: generic list mirroring KEDA ScaledObject spec.triggers.
    # Each entry is passed verbatim into the ScaledObject — supports any KEDA trigger type:
    # azure-service-bus, azure-cosmosdb, azure-blob, azure-queue, azure-eventhub, etc.
    # The connectionFromEnv field should reference the env var that holds the connection string.
    triggers: []
    # Examples:
    # Service Bus topic:
    # - type: azure-service-bus
    #   metadata:
    #     topicName: "emergenciestopic"
    #     subscriptionName: "webhook-incident-emergencies"
    #     connectionFromEnv: AzureWebJobsServiceBus
    # CosmosDB change feed:
    # - type: azure-cosmosdb
    #   metadata:
    #     endpoint: "https://my-cosmos.documents.azure.com:443/"
    #     databaseName: "MyDb"
    #     containerName: "MyContainer"
    #     leaseContainerName: "leases"
    #     connectionFromEnv: CosmosDbConnectionString
    # Azure Blob Storage:
    # - type: azure-blob
    #   metadata:
    #     blobContainerName: "my-container"
    #     connectionFromEnv: AzureWebJobsStorage

# --- Existing Configuration (Auto-populated) ---
existing:
  env_vars: []                # Environment variables found in current manifests
  secrets: []                 # Secret references found
  resource_limits: {}         # Current resource limits
  files_to_delete: []         # Files that will be removed (old k8s/, .gitlab-ci/, etc.)

# --- Pipeline Template Versions ---
pipeline:
  templates_version: "v5"
  kustomize_deploy_version: "v5"
  apim_version: "v5"
```

## Field Reference

### Required Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `service.name` | string | Kubernetes service/deployment name |
| `service.app_name` | string | Short application name used in namespaces |
| `service.display_name` | string | Full .NET assembly display name |
| `service.project_path` | string | Relative path to API project directory |
| `service.dll_path` | string | Main DLL filename |
| `jira.ticket` | string | Jira ticket for the migration work |
| `regions` | list | List of deployment regions |
| `region_config` | map | Per-region, per-environment infrastructure config |

### Decision Tree Fields

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `decisions.api_versioning.multi_version` | bool | `false` | Whether service exposes multiple API versions |
| `decisions.api_versioning.versions` | list | `[v1]` | API versions exposed |
| `decisions.auth0.enabled` | bool | `false` | Whether Auth0 deployment is needed |
| `decisions.nuget.enabled` | bool | `false` | Whether NuGet packages are published |
| `decisions.nuget.packages` | list | `[]` | Package project suffixes to publish |
| `decisions.ui_tests.enabled` | bool | `false` | Whether UI regression tests exist |
| `decisions.ui_tests.trigger_project` | string | `""` | GitLab project path for QA automation trigger |
| `decisions.ui_tests.schedule_name` | string | `""` | QA framework schedule name for staging tests |
| `decisions.ui_tests.schedule_name_prod` | string | `""` | QA framework schedule name for prod tests |
| `decisions.ef_migrations.enabled` | bool | `false` | Whether EF Core migrations exist |
| `decisions.ef_migrations.db_context_name` | string | `""` | EF Core DbContext class name |
| `decisions.ef_migrations.connection_string_key` | string | `""` | Azure App Config connection string key |

### Optional Fields

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `kubernetes.health_check_path` | string | `/v1/health` | Health endpoint path |
| `kubernetes.port` | int | `8080` | Container port |
| `kubernetes.resources` | map | See schema | CPU/memory limits and requests |
| `gateway.name` | string | `gateway` | Gateway resource name for HTTPRoutes |
| `gateway.namespace` | string | `azure-alb` | Gateway resource namespace |
| `review.domain` | string | `review.trigent.com` | Review app base domain |
| `pipeline.templates_version` | string | `v5` | Pipeline templates Git ref |

## Example: Client Building Service

```yaml
service:
  name: "clientbuilding-api"
  app_name: "clientbuilding"
  display_name: "Trigent.Platform.ClientBuildingService.Api"
  project_path: "src/Trigent.Platform.ClientBuildingService.Api"
  dll_path: "Trigent.Platform.ClientBuildingService.Api.dll"
  solution_file: "Trigent.Platform.ClientBuildingService.sln"

jira:
  ticket: "PLT-2763"

regions:
  - us
  - uk

region_config:
  us:
    domain: "trigent.com"
    dev:
      acr: "devcracrregistrytj"
      cluster: "dev-aks-akscluster-re3-001-yn"
      cluster_rg: "dev-rg-re3-mu"
      appconfig: "trigent-plt-app-config-dev-dr"
    staging:
      acr: "stagcracrregistrytj"
      cluster: "stag-aks-akscluster-re3-001"
      cluster_rg: "stag-rg-re3-mu"
      appconfig: "trigent-plt-app-config-stag-dr"
    prod:
      acr: "prodcracrregistrytj"
      cluster: "prod-aks-akscluster-re3-001"
      cluster_rg: "prod-rg-re3-mu"
      appconfig: "trigent-plt-app-config-prod-dr"
  uk:
    domain: "cpoms.net"
    dev:
      acr: "devukcracrregistrytj"
      cluster: "devuk-aks-akscluster-re3-001"
      cluster_rg: "devuk-rg-re3-mu"
      appconfig: "trigentuk-plt-app-config-dev"
    staging:
      acr: "stagukcracrregistrytj"
      cluster: "staguk-aks-akscluster-re3-001"
      cluster_rg: "staguk-rg-re3-mu"
      appconfig: "trigentuk-plt-app-config-stag"
    prod:
      acr: "produkcracrregistrytj"
      cluster: "produk-aks-akscluster-re3-001"
      cluster_rg: "produk-rg-re3-mu"
      appconfig: "trigentuk-plt-app-config-prod"

gateway:
  name: "gateway"
  namespace: "azure-alb"

decisions:
  api_versioning:
    multi_version: true
    versions: [v1, v2]
    api_ids:
      us-dev-v1: "abc123..."
      us-dev-v2: "def456..."
      us-staging-v1: "..."
      # ... one entry per region/environment/version combination
  auth0:
    enabled: true
  nuget:
    enabled: true
    packages: ["Client", "Shared", "Maps"]
  ui_tests:
    enabled: true
    trigger_project: "trigent1/trigent/quality-assurance/qa-automation/web-ui-framework-2-platform"
    schedule_name: "CICD - Client Building Service - P0,P1 - Staging"
    schedule_name_prod: "CICD - Client Building Service - P0,P1 - Prod"
  ef_migrations:
    enabled: true
    db_context_name: "ClientsDBContext"
    connection_string_key: "ClientsDB_Failover"
    data_project_path: "src/Trigent.Platform.ClientBuildingService.DataMigrations/Trigent.Platform.ClientBuildingService.DataMigrations.csproj"
```

---

## Multi-Service Schema

For repos containing multiple deployable services (any combination of types — multiple workers, multiple HTTP functions, workers with different triggers, etc.), replace the single `service:` block with a `services:` list. All other top-level fields (`jira`, `regions`, `region_config`, `kubernetes`, `gateway`, `review`, `decisions`, `pipeline`) are shared.

```yaml
# Replace `service:` with `services:` for multi-service repos
services:
  - name: "SERVICE_NAME_1"              # K8s deployment name — MUST match existing to avoid orphans
    app_name: "APP_NAME_1"
    display_name: "Full.Assembly.Name.One"
    project_path: "src/ProjectOne"
    dll_path: "Full.Assembly.Name.One.dll"
    type: "functions-worker"            # "functions-worker" | "functions-http" | "webapi"
    functions:
      secret_name: "service-one-secrets"
      service_bus_secret_name: "ConnectionStrings--myServiceBusKey"  # empty if not Service Bus
      keda:
        enabled: true
        triggers:
          - type: azure-service-bus     # Any KEDA trigger type — passed verbatim to ScaledObject
            metadata:
              topicName: "my-topic"
              subscriptionName: "my-subscription"
              connectionFromEnv: AzureWebJobsServiceBus
          # Additional triggers of any type (cosmosdb, blob, queue, eventhub, etc.)
          - type: azure-cosmosdb
            metadata:
              endpoint: "https://my-cosmos.documents.azure.com:443/"
              databaseName: "MyDb"
              containerName: "MyContainer"
              leaseContainerName: "leases"
              connectionFromEnv: CosmosDbConnectionString

  - name: "SERVICE_NAME_2"              # Any type — same or different from other entries
    app_name: "APP_NAME_2"
    display_name: "Full.Assembly.Name.Two"
    project_path: "src/ProjectTwo"
    dll_path: "Full.Assembly.Name.Two.dll"
    type: "functions-http"
    functions:
      secret_name: "service-two-secrets"
      service_bus_secret_name: ""
      keda:
        enabled: false
        triggers: []

  # Add as many services as the repo contains — no limit on count or type combination

# New Relic: explicit per-service names for EVERY region/env the service deploys to.
# IMPORTANT: populate from the actual live NEW_RELIC_APP_NAME values in current k8s manifests
# or CI pipeline variables — not from the display_name pattern — to preserve existing
# dashboards and alert policies.
# Keys follow the pattern "{region}-{env}". Suffix convention:
#   us-dev → "-dev"  |  us-staging → "-stag"  |  us-prod → "-prod"
#   uk-dev → "-devuk" | uk-staging → "-staguk" | uk-prod → "-produk"
# Only include keys for region/env combinations that exist in `regions:` + `region_config:`.
new_relic:
  app_name_pattern: "{display_name}-{suffix}"   # Fallback if app_names not set
  app_names:
    SERVICE_NAME_1:
      us-dev: "Full.Assembly.Name.One-dev"
      us-staging: "Full.Assembly.Name.One-stag"
      us-prod: "Full.Assembly.Name.One-prod"
      uk-dev: "Full.Assembly.Name.One-devuk"     # Include only if UK region is in regions:
      uk-staging: "Full.Assembly.Name.One-staguk"
      uk-prod: "Full.Assembly.Name.One-produk"
    SERVICE_NAME_2:
      us-dev: "Full.Assembly.Name.Two-dev"
      us-staging: "Full.Assembly.Name.Two-stag"
      us-prod: "Full.Assembly.Name.Two-prod"
      # Omit uk-* if SERVICE_NAME_2 does not deploy to UK
```

### Multi-Service Pipeline Behaviour

When `services:` is present, `/dotnet:trunk-migrate` generates:

- **One kustomize base** (`k8s/base/`) listing all service deployments — reference: `email-function`
- **One shared overlay** per environment — patches all services together
- **One `.gitlab-ci.yml`** with `docker-build-job` parallel matrix (one row per service)
- **One review app** using `image-names: svc1,svc2,...` and `deployment-names: svc1,svc2,...`
- **Per-service K8s secrets** created at deploy time from each service's `functions.secret_name`
- **Region kustomizations** patch each service's `NEW_RELIC_APP_NAME` using the correct `app_names` entry for that region/env

### Container Name Preservation Rule

The `name:` in each service entry **must exactly match** the existing `metadata.name` of the Kubernetes Deployment. This ensures `kubectl apply` overwrites the existing deployment rather than creating a parallel one, preventing orphaned workloads.
